dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - April 24, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**MLB Recap: April 24th**

The Cincinnati Reds outlasted the Detroit Tigers 9-8, thanks in part to Matt McLain's 2-home run, 4-RBI performance. McLain's big day included two hits in four at-bats, leading the Reds to their first win in a back-and-forth contest. Meanwhile, on the opposite coast, the Baltimore Orioles dominated the Boston Red Sox 10-3, with Adley Rutschman driving in six runs on three hits, including two home runs. Rutschman was nearly unstoppable, finishing 3-for-5 with a pair of long balls.

In other action, Freddy Peralta tossed 5.2 scoreless innings for the Colorado Rockies, striking out eight in a 4-3 win over the New York Mets. The Minnesota Twins were no match for Drew Rasmussen, who threw six scoreless innings for the Tampa Bay Rays in a 6-2 victory. The Pittsburgh Pirates also shut out the Milwaukee Brewers 6-0, with Paul Skenes pitching a complete game shutout, allowing just one hit and striking out seven.
